slides.title: PhD CET Mock Exam 9 - Computer Science
slides.subtitle: 50 Practice MCQs for Doctoral Entrance Examination • Topic 1-5

slides.question: Q1: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Vertex Degree**?
options: It is equivalent in purpose to: is a computer design model featuring a single shared memory and bus for both instruction and data storage.
It is fundamentally characterized by: is a combinational logic circuit that selects binary information from one of many input lines and directs it to a single output.
It has no relation to: vertex incident edge count
It is correctly described as: is the number of edges incident to a vertex in a graph, with self-loops counting twice.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Vertex Degree** is accurately described as: is the number of edges incident to a vertex in a graph, with self-loops counting twice.

slides.question: Q2: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Euler Path**?
options: It is equivalent in purpose to: is a multi-level layout (L1, L2, L3) of small, fast memory to bridge the speed gap between CPU and RAM.
It has no relation to: single-visit edge traversal trail
It is correctly described as: is a trail in a finite graph that visits every edge exactly once, existing if exactly zero or two vertices have odd degree.
It is fundamentally characterized by: is a combinational logic circuit that takes a single input line and routes it to one of several digital outputs.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Euler Path** is accurately described as: is a trail in a finite graph that visits every edge exactly once, existing if exactly zero or two vertices have odd degree.

slides.question: Q3: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Hamiltonian Cycle**?
options: It is equivalent in purpose to: allows an I/O device to transfer data directly to or from the system memory without passing through the CPU.
It is fundamentally characterized by: is a combinational circuit that adds three single-bit inputs (A, B, and Carry-in) and outputs Sum and Carry.
It has no relation to: single-visit vertex traversal loop
It is correctly described as: is a closed loop in a graph that visits every vertex exactly once and returns to the starting vertex.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Hamiltonian Cycle** is accurately described as: is a closed loop in a graph that visits every vertex exactly once and returns to the starting vertex.

slides.question: Q4: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Group Theory Group**?
options: It has no relation to: closure-associativity-identity-inverse algebraic structure
It is fundamentally characterized by: are sequential logic circuits used as basic memory storage elements, changing states based on clock inputs (e.g. JK, D).
It is equivalent in purpose to: are situations in CPU execution (structural, data, control) that prevent the next instruction from executing in its designated cycle.
It is correctly described as: is a set combined with a binary operation satisfying closure, associativity, identity, and invertibility.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Group Theory Group** is accurately described as: is a set combined with a binary operation satisfying closure, associativity, identity, and invertibility.

slides.question: Q5: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Permutation**?
options: It is equivalent in purpose to: consists of the sequence of actions: Fetch, Decode, Read effective address, Execute, and Write-back.
It is fundamentally characterized by: is a digital counter where all flip-flops are triggered simultaneously by a single common clock pulse.
It has no relation to: order-sensitive selection arrangement
It is correctly described as: is an ordered arrangement of all or part of a set of objects, where the order of selection matters.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Permutation** is accurately described as: is an ordered arrangement of all or part of a set of objects, where the order of selection matters.

slides.question: Q6: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Boolean Algebra Minimization**?
options: It has no relation to: logic gate reduction optimization
It is correctly described as: is the process of simplifying a Boolean expression to reduce the number of logic gates required for implementation.
It is equivalent in purpose to: features a small set of simple, uniform, single-cycle execution instructions to optimize compiler efficiency.
It is fundamentally characterized by: is a combinational circuit that converts an n-bit binary input code into a maximum of 2^n unique output lines.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Boolean Algebra Minimization** is accurately described as: is the process of simplifying a Boolean expression to reduce the number of logic gates required for implementation.

slides.question: Q7: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Karnaugh Map (K-map)**?
options: It has no relation to: graphical boolean simplification table
It is equivalent in purpose to: is the widely adopted technical standard for floating-point computation, defining sign, exponent, and mantissa formats.
It is correctly described as: is a graphical method used to simplify Boolean algebra expressions without using algebraic laws.
It is fundamentally characterized by: is a computer design model featuring a single shared memory and bus for both instruction and data storage.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Karnaugh Map (K-map)** is accurately described as: is a graphical method used to simplify Boolean algebra expressions without using algebraic laws.

slides.question: Q8: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Multiplexer (MUX)**?
options: It is equivalent in purpose to: are symbols used to connect propositions to form compound propositions, such as AND, OR, and NOT.
It is correctly described as: is a combinational logic circuit that selects binary information from one of many input lines and directs it to a single output.
It has no relation to: many-to-one signal selector
It is fundamentally characterized by: is a multi-level layout (L1, L2, L3) of small, fast memory to bridge the speed gap between CPU and RAM.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Multiplexer (MUX)** is accurately described as: is a combinational logic circuit that selects binary information from one of many input lines and directs it to a single output.

slides.question: Q9: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Demultiplexer (DEMUX)**?
options: It is fundamentally characterized by: allows an I/O device to transfer data directly to or from the system memory without passing through the CPU.
It is equivalent in purpose to: are symbols like Universal (forall) and Existential (exists) that specify the scope of a predicate's variables.
It is correctly described as: is a combinational logic circuit that takes a single input line and routes it to one of several digital outputs.
It has no relation to: one-to-many signal router
slides.explanation: Correct! **Demultiplexer (DEMUX)** is accurately described as: is a combinational logic circuit that takes a single input line and routes it to one of several digital outputs.

slides.question: Q10: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Full Adder**?
options: It is equivalent in purpose to: include Union, Intersection, Difference, and Complement used to manipulate and combine collections of objects.
It is fundamentally characterized by: are situations in CPU execution (structural, data, control) that prevent the next instruction from executing in its designated cycle.
It has no relation to: three-input binary addition circuit
It is correctly described as: is a combinational circuit that adds three single-bit inputs (A, B, and Carry-in) and outputs Sum and Carry.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Full Adder** is accurately described as: is a combinational circuit that adds three single-bit inputs (A, B, and Carry-in) and outputs Sum and Carry.

slides.question: Q11: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Paging**?
options: It is correctly described as: is a memory management scheme that eliminates the need for contiguous physical memory allocation by dividing logical memory into blocks.
It has no relation to: elimination of external fragmentation
It is fundamentally characterized by: is a synchronization tool that uses a signaling integer value to control access to shared resources by multiple processes.
It is equivalent in purpose to: provide the essential interface between a running program and the operating system kernel to request privileged services.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Paging** is accurately described as: is a memory management scheme that eliminates the need for contiguous physical memory allocation by dividing logical memory into blocks.

slides.question: Q12: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Virtual Memory**?
options: It has no relation to: logical memory expansion
It is correctly described as: is a technique that allows the execution of processes that are not completely in physical memory, using disk storage.
It is equivalent in purpose to: designate different configurations of multiple physical disks to achieve redundancy, performance, or both (e.g. RAID 0, 1, 5).
It is fundamentally characterized by: is the process of storing the state of a CPU process/thread so that it can be restored and resume execution later.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Virtual Memory** is accurately described as: is a technique that allows the execution of processes that are not completely in physical memory, using disk storage.

slides.question: Q13: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Translation Lookaside Buffer (TLB)**?
options: It is correctly described as: is a hardware cache memory that stores recent translations of virtual memory addresses to physical memory addresses.
It is equivalent in purpose to: minimizes the kernel size by moving non-essential services (like file systems, drivers) into user space.
It is fundamentally characterized by: refers to mechanisms (like pipes, message queues, shared memory) that allow processes to communicate and share data.
It has no relation to: fast page table lookup cache
slides.explanation: Correct! **Translation Lookaside Buffer (TLB)** is accurately described as: is a hardware cache memory that stores recent translations of virtual memory addresses to physical memory addresses.

slides.question: Q14: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Page Replacement Algorithm**?
options: It is fundamentally characterized by: uses temporary memory areas to store data during transfers between fast devices (like CPU) and slow I/O peripherals.
It is equivalent in purpose to: ensure that data changes in one local cache are correctly propagated to other caches in a multiprocessor system.
It has no relation to: physical memory page eviction policy
It is correctly described as: decides which memory page to swap out of physical RAM when a new page needs to be allocated (e.g. LRU, FIFO).
slides.explanation: Correct! **Page Replacement Algorithm** is accurately described as: decides which memory page to swap out of physical RAM when a new page needs to be allocated (e.g. LRU, FIFO).

slides.question: Q15: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Thrashing**?
options: It is fundamentally characterized by: is the process of managing I/O requests for a hard disk drive using algorithms like SCAN, C-SCAN, or SSTF.
It is equivalent in purpose to: is a memory management technique that divides virtual memory into variable-sized segments based on logical modules.
It is correctly described as: is a state where the operating system spends more time swapping pages in and out of disk than executing instructions.
It has no relation to: excessive paging performance collapse
slides.explanation: Correct! **Thrashing** is accurately described as: is a state where the operating system spends more time swapping pages in and out of disk than executing instructions.

slides.question: Q16: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Thread**?
options: It is fundamentally characterized by: include Contiguous, Linked, and Indexed allocation strategies for storing files on physical storage devices.
It is equivalent in purpose to: is a specialized software program that controls a specific hardware device attached to the computer.
It is correctly described as: is the smallest unit of execution within a process, sharing the process's memory space but maintaining its own stack and registers.
It has no relation to: lightweight shared-memory execution unit
slides.explanation: Correct! **Thread** is accurately described as: is the smallest unit of execution within a process, sharing the process's memory space but maintaining its own stack and registers.

slides.question: Q17: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Mutex**?
options: It is equivalent in purpose to: is an acronym for Simultaneous Peripheral Operations On-Line, storing printer/device jobs in a buffer to execute sequentially.
It is fundamentally characterized by: provide the essential interface between a running program and the operating system kernel to request privileged services.
It has no relation to: single-thread resource lock
It is correctly described as: is a mutual exclusion locking mechanism used to synchronize access to a resource, ensuring only one thread holds the lock at a time.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Mutex** is accurately described as: is a mutual exclusion locking mechanism used to synchronize access to a resource, ensuring only one thread holds the lock at a time.

slides.question: Q18: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Counting Semaphore**?
options: It has no relation to: variable-based process signaling
It is correctly described as: is a synchronization tool that uses a signaling integer value to control access to shared resources by multiple processes.
It is fundamentally characterized by: designate different configurations of multiple physical disks to achieve redundancy, performance, or both (e.g. RAID 0, 1, 5).
It is equivalent in purpose to: include Ready, Running, Waiting, New, and Terminated, representing the life-cycle stages of a process.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Counting Semaphore** is accurately described as: is a synchronization tool that uses a signaling integer value to control access to shared resources by multiple processes.

slides.question: Q19: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Context Switching**?
options: It is correctly described as: is the process of storing the state of a CPU process/thread so that it can be restored and resume execution later.
It has no relation to: cpu state saving-restoring overhead
It is equivalent in purpose to: is the process of allocating CPU time to processes in the ready queue using algorithms like Round Robin or SJF.
It is fundamentally characterized by: minimizes the kernel size by moving non-essential services (like file systems, drivers) into user space.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Context Switching** is accurately described as: is the process of storing the state of a CPU process/thread so that it can be restored and resume execution later.

slides.question: Q20: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Inter-Process Communication (IPC)**?
options: It is fundamentally characterized by: ensure that data changes in one local cache are correctly propagated to other caches in a multiprocessor system.
It is equivalent in purpose to: coordinates the execution of concurrent processes to ensure no two processes access shared data simultaneously.
It is correctly described as: refers to mechanisms (like pipes, message queues, shared memory) that allow processes to communicate and share data.
It has no relation to: isolated process communication mechanisms
slides.explanation: Correct! **Inter-Process Communication (IPC)** is accurately described as: refers to mechanisms (like pipes, message queues, shared memory) that allow processes to communicate and share data.

slides.question: Q21: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **AVL Tree**?
options: It is equivalent in purpose to: is the programming operation used to retrieve or modify the value stored at the memory address held by a pointer.
It has no relation to: strictly balanced binary search tree
It is fundamentally characterized by: is a linear data structure that follows the First-In-First-Out (FIFO) principle for inserting and removing elements.
It is correctly described as: is a self-balancing binary search tree where the heights of the two child subtrees of any node differ by at most one.
slides.explanation: Correct! **AVL Tree** is accurately described as: is a self-balancing binary search tree where the heights of the two child subtrees of any node differ by at most one.

slides.question: Q22: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Quick Sort**?
options: It has no relation to: in-place sorting with O(n log n) average time
It is correctly described as: is a divide-and-conquer sorting algorithm that selects a pivot element and partitions the array around it.
It is fundamentally characterized by: is a linear collection of data nodes where each node points to the next node in the sequence.
It is equivalent in purpose to: is an object-oriented programming concept allowing objects of different classes to be treated as objects of a common superclass.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Quick Sort** is accurately described as: is a divide-and-conquer sorting algorithm that selects a pivot element and partitions the array around it.

slides.question: Q23: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Adjacency List**?
options: It has no relation to: space-efficient sparse graph representation
It is correctly described as: represents a graph as an array of linked lists, where each index represents a vertex and its list contains its adjacent neighbors.
It is fundamentally characterized by: is a linear collection of nodes where each node contains pointers to both the next and the previous nodes.
It is equivalent in purpose to: is an OOP mechanism where a new class (derived) adopts the attributes and behaviors of an existing class (base).
slides.explanation: Correct! **Adjacency List** is accurately described as: represents a graph as an array of linked lists, where each index represents a vertex and its list contains its adjacent neighbors.

slides.question: Q24: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Breadth-First Search (BFS)**?
options: It is equivalent in purpose to: is the OOP practice of bundling data and methods that operate on that data within a single unit, restricting direct access.
It has no relation to: shortest path in unweighted graphs
It is correctly described as: traverses a graph level by level, utilizing a queue data structure to visit vertices in order of increasing distance.
It is fundamentally characterized by: is a stable, divide-and-conquer sorting algorithm that splits arrays in halves, sorts them, and merges them back.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Breadth-First Search (BFS)** is accurately described as: traverses a graph level by level, utilizing a queue data structure to visit vertices in order of increasing distance.

slides.question: Q25: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Depth-First Search (DFS)**?
options: It is equivalent in purpose to: is a mathematical model for data types defined by its behavior (operations) from the point of view of a user, not implementation.
It is fundamentally characterized by: is an efficient search algorithm that repeatedly divides a sorted search interval in half to locate a target.
It has no relation to: connectivity and topological sorting tool
It is correctly described as: traverses a graph by exploring as deep as possible along each branch before backtracking, utilizing a stack.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Depth-First Search (DFS)** is accurately described as: traverses a graph by exploring as deep as possible along each branch before backtracking, utilizing a stack.

slides.question: Q26: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Big O Complexity**?
options: It is fundamentally characterized by: is a stack memory structure that stores information about active subroutines and functions in a running program.
It has no relation to: asymptotic run-time upper bound indicator
It is equivalent in purpose to: is an automatic memory management process that reclaims heap memory occupied by objects that are no longer in use.
It is correctly described as: describes the asymptotic upper bound of an algorithm's execution time or space requirement in terms of input size.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Big O Complexity** is accurately described as: describes the asymptotic upper bound of an algorithm's execution time or space requirement in terms of input size.

slides.question: Q27: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Stack Data Structure**?
options: It is correctly described as: is a linear data structure that follows the Last-In-First-Out (LIFO) principle for inserting and removing elements.
It is fundamentally characterized by: is the programming operation used to retrieve or modify the value stored at the memory address held by a pointer.
It is equivalent in purpose to: defines the region of program source code text within which a variable's binding or name is valid and accessible.
It has no relation to: LIFO element storage collection
slides.explanation: Correct! **Stack Data Structure** is accurately described as: is a linear data structure that follows the Last-In-First-Out (LIFO) principle for inserting and removing elements.

slides.question: Q28: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Queue Data Structure**?
options: It has no relation to: FIFO element storage collection
It is correctly described as: is a linear data structure that follows the First-In-First-Out (FIFO) principle for inserting and removing elements.
It is fundamentally characterized by: is an object-oriented programming concept allowing objects of different classes to be treated as objects of a common superclass.
It is equivalent in purpose to: is a node-based binary tree data structure where the left subtree contains keys less than the parent, and the right contains keys greater.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Queue Data Structure** is accurately described as: is a linear data structure that follows the First-In-First-Out (FIFO) principle for inserting and removing elements.

slides.question: Q29: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Singly Linked List**?
options: It is correctly described as: is a linear collection of data nodes where each node points to the next node in the sequence.
It is fundamentally characterized by: is an OOP mechanism where a new class (derived) adopts the attributes and behaviors of an existing class (base).
It has no relation to: one-way pointer node sequence
It is equivalent in purpose to: finds the shortest path from a single source vertex to all other vertices in a weighted graph with non-negative edge weights.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Singly Linked List** is accurately described as: is a linear collection of data nodes where each node points to the next node in the sequence.

slides.question: Q30: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Doubly Linked List**?
options: It is fundamentally characterized by: is the OOP practice of bundling data and methods that operate on that data within a single unit, restricting direct access.
It is correctly described as: is a linear collection of nodes where each node contains pointers to both the next and the previous nodes.
It has no relation to: two-way pointer node sequence
It is equivalent in purpose to: solves complex problems by breaking them down into simpler subproblems and storing the results of these subproblems to avoid redundant computing.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Doubly Linked List** is accurately described as: is a linear collection of nodes where each node contains pointers to both the next and the previous nodes.

slides.question: Q31: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Foreign Key**?
options: It has no relation to: referential integrity constraint
It is equivalent in purpose to: is a DBMS component that analyzes SQL statements and determines the most efficient execution plan based on statistics.
It is fundamentally characterized by: represents the logical structure of a database using Entities, Attributes, and Relationships visually.
It is correctly described as: is a field in a relational database table that uniquely identifies a row in another table, enforcing referential integrity.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Foreign Key** is accurately described as: is a field in a relational database table that uniquely identifies a row in another table, enforcing referential integrity.

slides.question: Q32: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **B+ Tree Index**?
options: It is correctly described as: is a self-balancing search tree structure widely used in databases to enable fast sequential access and range queries.
It is fundamentally characterized by: reverts all changes made during the current transaction if a failure occurs before completion, ensuring atomicity.
It is equivalent in purpose to: is a procedural block of code that automatically executes in response to certain events on a particular table.
It has no relation to: disk-optimized indexing structure
slides.explanation: Correct! **B+ Tree Index** is accurately described as: is a self-balancing search tree structure widely used in databases to enable fast sequential access and range queries.

slides.question: Q33: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Two-Phase Locking (2PL)**?
options: It is fundamentally characterized by: occurs when two concurrent transactions read and update the same data item, where one overwrite is lost.
It is equivalent in purpose to: is a precompiled collection of SQL statements stored in the database, reducing network traffic and improving performance.
It has no relation to: serializability guarantee in transactions
It is correctly described as: is a concurrency control method that guarantees serializability by locking data items in growing and shrinking phases.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Two-Phase Locking (2PL)** is accurately described as: is a concurrency control method that guarantees serializability by locking data items in growing and shrinking phases.

slides.question: Q34: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **SQL JOIN**?
options: It is fundamentally characterized by: occurs when a transaction reads data that has been modified by another concurrent transaction but not yet committed.
It has no relation to: multi-table data retrieval operation
It is correctly described as: combines records from two or more tables in a relational database based on a related column between them.
It is equivalent in purpose to: is a column or group of columns that uniquely identifies each row in a database table, and cannot contain NULL values.
slides.explanation: Correct! **SQL JOIN** is accurately described as: combines records from two or more tables in a relational database based on a related column between them.

slides.question: Q35: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Relational Algebra**?
options: It has no relation to: theoretical query language foundation
It is fundamentally characterized by: occurs when a transaction re-reads data it previously read and finds that another committed transaction has modified it.
It is correctly described as: is a procedural query language consisting of operations like Select, Project, Join, and Union to manipulate relations.
It is equivalent in purpose to: is a minimal superkey that can uniquely identify any database record without redundant attributes.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Relational Algebra** is accurately described as: is a procedural query language consisting of operations like Select, Project, Join, and Union to manipulate relations.

slides.question: Q36: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Database View**?
options: It is correctly described as: is a virtual table based on the result-set of an active SQL statement, containing no physical data of its own.
It has no relation to: virtual table query abstraction
It is equivalent in purpose to: is a state where all foreign key values in a database are valid, pointing to existing primary keys in parent tables.
It is fundamentally characterized by: occurs when a transaction re-runs a query returning a set of rows and finds new rows added by another committed transaction.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Database View** is accurately described as: is a virtual table based on the result-set of an active SQL statement, containing no physical data of its own.

slides.question: Q37: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **NoSQL Database**?
options: It has no relation to: non-relational schema-less storage
It is fundamentally characterized by: is a DBMS component that analyzes SQL statements and determines the most efficient execution plan based on statistics.
It is correctly described as: provides a mechanism for storage and retrieval of data modeled in formats other than tabular relations (e.g. document, key-value).
It is equivalent in purpose to: occurs when the same piece of data is stored in two or more separate places in a database unnecessarily.
slides.explanation: Correct! **NoSQL Database** is accurately described as: provides a mechanism for storage and retrieval of data modeled in formats other than tabular relations (e.g. document, key-value).

slides.question: Q38: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **ER Modeling**?
options: It has no relation to: logical database structure visualization
It is correctly described as: represents the logical structure of a database using Entities, Attributes, and Relationships visually.
It is equivalent in purpose to: requires that domain values must be atomic, meaning attributes cannot contain multiple or nested values.
It is fundamentally characterized by: is a procedural block of code that automatically executes in response to certain events on a particular table.
slides.explanation: Correct! **ER Modeling** is accurately described as: represents the logical structure of a database using Entities, Attributes, and Relationships visually.

slides.question: Q39: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Transaction Rollback**?
options: It is fundamentally characterized by: is a precompiled collection of SQL statements stored in the database, reducing network traffic and improving performance.
It is equivalent in purpose to: requires a table to be in 1NF and ensure that all non-prime attributes are fully functionally dependent on the primary key.
It has no relation to: reverting uncommitted transactional modifications
It is correctly described as: reverts all changes made during the current transaction if a failure occurs before completion, ensuring atomicity.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Transaction Rollback** is accurately described as: reverts all changes made during the current transaction if a failure occurs before completion, ensuring atomicity.

slides.question: Q40: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Lost Update Anomaly**?
options: It is correctly described as: occurs when two concurrent transactions read and update the same data item, where one overwrite is lost.
It is equivalent in purpose to: requires a table to be in 2NF and ensure that no non-prime attribute is transitively dependent on the primary key.
It is fundamentally characterized by: is a column or group of columns that uniquely identifies each row in a database table, and cannot contain NULL values.
It has no relation to: concurrent write overwrite anomaly
slides.explanation: Correct! **Lost Update Anomaly** is accurately described as: occurs when two concurrent transactions read and update the same data item, where one overwrite is lost.

slides.question: Q41: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Asymmetric Encryption**?
options: It is equivalent in purpose to: is an agile framework for managing complex work, utilizing sprints, daily standups, and product backlogs.
It has no relation to: public-private key pair cryptography
It is correctly described as: uses a mathematically linked public-private key pair for encryption and decryption operations.
It is fundamentally characterized by: is a software testing method by which individual source code components are tested to determine if they are fit for use.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Asymmetric Encryption** is accurately described as: uses a mathematically linked public-private key pair for encryption and decryption operations.

slides.question: Q42: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **DNS (Domain Name System)**?
options: It is fundamentally characterized by: is a distributed version control system designed to track changes in source code during software development.
It has no relation to: internet address resolution directory
It is correctly described as: translates human-readable domain names into machine-readable IP addresses.
It is equivalent in purpose to: measures the strength of relationship between pieces of functionality within a single software module.
slides.explanation: Correct! **DNS (Domain Name System)** is accurately described as: translates human-readable domain names into machine-readable IP addresses.

slides.question: Q43: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **ARP (Address Resolution Protocol)**?
options: It is fundamentally characterized by: automates software delivery phases: building, testing, and deploying changes to production environments.
It has no relation to: IP to MAC address resolver
It is equivalent in purpose to: measures the degree of interdependence between different software modules, aiming for a low level to increase modularity.
It is correctly described as: is a protocol used to map an Internet Protocol (IP) address to a physical machine Media Access Control (MAC) address.
slides.explanation: Correct! **ARP (Address Resolution Protocol)** is accurately described as: is a protocol used to map an Internet Protocol (IP) address to a physical machine Media Access Control (MAC) address.

slides.question: Q44: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Firewall**?
options: It is correctly described as: is a network security device that monitors and filters incoming and outgoing network traffic based on established security rules.
It is equivalent in purpose to: is a structured process used by the software industry to design, develop, and test high-quality software (e.g., Waterfall).
It has no relation to: traffic filtering barrier
It is fundamentally characterized by: is a method of software testing that examines the functionality of an application without peering into its internal structures.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Firewall** is accurately described as: is a network security device that monitors and filters incoming and outgoing network traffic based on established security rules.

slides.question: Q45: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **IPv6 Address Space**?
options: It is correctly described as: provides 128-bit addresses to replace 32-bit IPv4 addresses, accommodating the massive growth of internet devices.
It is fundamentally characterized by: is a method of software testing that tests internal structures or workings of an application, requiring code visibility.
It is equivalent in purpose to: provide a standardized visual representation of software systems to describe classes, objects, and their relationships.
It has no relation to: 128-bit internet address space standard
slides.explanation: Correct! **IPv6 Address Space** is accurately described as: provides 128-bit addresses to replace 32-bit IPv4 addresses, accommodating the massive growth of internet devices.

slides.question: Q46: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Agile Methodology**?
options: It is equivalent in purpose to: is a formal document describing the functional and non-functional requirements that the software system must fulfill.
It is fundamentally characterized by: refers to the high-level structures of a software system, the discipline of creating such structures.
It is correctly described as: promotes continuous iteration of development and testing throughout the software development life cycle.
It has no relation to: flexible iterative development paradigm
slides.explanation: Correct! **Agile Methodology** is accurately described as: promotes continuous iteration of development and testing throughout the software development life cycle.

slides.question: Q47: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Design Pattern**?
options: It is correctly described as: is a reusable, template-like solution to a commonly occurring design problem in software architecture.
It has no relation to: reusable architectural solution blueprint
It is equivalent in purpose to: is the process of restructuring existing computer code without changing its external behavior, to improve readability and structure.
It is fundamentally characterized by: is an agile framework for managing complex work, utilizing sprints, daily standups, and product backlogs.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Design Pattern** is accurately described as: is a reusable, template-like solution to a commonly occurring design problem in software architecture.

slides.question: Q48: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Unit Testing**?
options: It is correctly described as: is a software testing method by which individual source code components are tested to determine if they are fit for use.
It has no relation to: isolated component validation
It is fundamentally characterized by: measures the strength of relationship between pieces of functionality within a single software module.
It is equivalent in purpose to: is a conceptual framework of 7 layers that standardizes telecommunication and networking protocol functions.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Unit Testing** is accurately described as: is a software testing method by which individual source code components are tested to determine if they are fit for use.

slides.question: Q49: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Git Version Control**?
options: It has no relation to: distributed source control management
It is fundamentally characterized by: measures the degree of interdependence between different software modules, aiming for a low level to increase modularity.
It is correctly described as: is a distributed version control system designed to track changes in source code during software development.
It is equivalent in purpose to: is a suite of communication protocols used to interconnect network devices on the internet, emphasizing reliable delivery.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Git Version Control** is accurately described as: is a distributed version control system designed to track changes in source code during software development.

slides.question: Q50: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **CI/CD Pipeline**?
options: It has no relation to: automated deployment automation
It is correctly described as: automates software delivery phases: building, testing, and deploying changes to production environments.
It is fundamentally characterized by: is a structured process used by the software industry to design, develop, and test high-quality software (e.g., Waterfall).
It is equivalent in purpose to: is a 32-bit number that masks an IP address, dividing the IP address into network address and host address portions.
slides.explanation: Correct! **CI/CD Pipeline** is accurately described as: automates software delivery phases: building, testing, and deploying changes to production environments.
